definition: ngram4's 4-edge path-tuple index distinguishes among many parallel dispatch alternatives on the false side of a gate (e.g. a content-type dispatch fan-out), rewarding lateral diversity across distinct content branches and retaining varied non-default seeds that single-edge feedback under-retains.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: ngram4 rewards the false-branch content-dispatch fan-out (END_TAG, PI, start-tag, char-data) as distinct 4-edge path tuples, retaining diverse non-CDATA seeds that single-edge feedback under-retains, keeping pressure off the CDATA true side.
signature.one_line: ngram4 path-tuples retain non-CDATA content seeds via false-branch fan-out

analysis.summary_one_line: Content-dispatch byte at cur[6] is NOT 'T' (input is not a '<![CDATA[' opener), so the parser falls through to other content branches.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: In XML_PARSER_CONTENT state, an input whose '<' lead bytes do NOT spell the CDATA opener '<![CDATA[' at the cursor (specifically cur[6] != 'T' on the '<![CDA...' prefix), driving the dispatch chain to its false side and into the sibling content branches (end-tag '</', PI '<?', start-tag '<X', '&' reference, char-data).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ngram4 winner seeds carry well-formed element/tag content (e.g. '<a>m', '<dtd', '<link:' fragments) that reach XML_PARSER_CONTENT and dispatch to end-tag and PI handling; the per-role overlay marks lines 11728-11735 (END_TAG / PI transitions) as winner-only [W] and the CDATA branch false, and the blocker line shows W T=0 F=12 (always takes the false side).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds concentrate on CDATA-opener-like and DOCTYPE prefixes (e.g. '<![C' fragments), so they enter the CDATA_SECTION arm; the overlay marks lines 11755-11757 and 11813+ as loser-only [L] and the blocker line shows L T=21 F=1 — the loser takes the true side and stalls inside the unterminated-CDATA scanning logic.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ngram4's NgramHook folds the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, so a previously-unseen 4-edge path tuple counts as new coverage. The content-dispatch cascade (cur=='<' then the next/cur[2..6] tests) produces many distinct short edge paths as the false side fans out to END_TAG/PI/start-tag/char-data; the n-gram feedback rewards seeds that exercise these path tuples, keeping diverse non-CDATA content seeds in the corpus that resolve the false branch. Plain naive (single-edge buckets) gives no extra credit for the path context, so it under-retains those seeds and stalls.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a byte-equality test cur[6]=='T' that is part of the '<![CDATA[' opener chain in XML_PARSER_CONTENT, and the globally-blocked side is the false branch.
The blocked side is the false branch.
The winner resolves the false branch and the loser is stuck on the true side at the blocker line.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L11752  T=0 F=12  T=21 F=1  (ctxt->input->cur[6] == 'T') &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-only coverage marks the end-tag transition path immediately after the content-dispatch false side.
Loser-only coverage marks the CDATA-section arm taken on the true side of the opener chain.
Winner seeds contain well-formed element/tag content reaching the content state.
Loser seeds carry CDATA-opener-like content fragments steering into the CDATA arm.
naive_ngram4 indexes the map by a length-4 edge path so a new 4-edge tuple is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
A map slot
therefore encodes a length-4 edge path (an n-gram of N=4) rather than
a single edge.

evidence_trail.claim: A new bucket under naive_ngram4 is a previously-unseen 4-edge path tuple, which rewards the content-dispatch fan-out.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new
bucket" is a previously-unseen 4-edge path tuple.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain naive uses single-edge hit-count buckets with no path context and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the content-dispatch cascade is collapsed to a single edge (no distinct 4-edge path tuples on the false-side branches) shows naive_ngram4 resolving the false branch no faster than plain naive — i.e., the n-gram advantage vanishes when path context is removed.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the winner's edge is genuinely the n-gram path-tuple credit on the content-dispatch fan-out rather than incidental corpus diversity from longer well-formed seeds; the prompt shows the coverage divergence but not the n-gram map slots directly.
